description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:17.09-git image with a docker:17.09-dind service, define the stages build, test, and deploy, and set the CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE variable to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME. In the build stage add a "build to homolog" job that runs only on the master branch, executes `docker run --rm -v $PWD:/app -w /app node:8 npm install`, then `docker run --rm -v $PWD:/app -w /app node:8 ./node_modules/.bin/ng build --prod --environment=homolog --output-path dist/empurrandojuntos --deploy-url /`, logs into the GitLab registry, creates an nginx container, copies the built files and nginx config into it, commits the container as $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME and pushes that tag. Also in the build stage add a "build to production" job that runs only on tag pushes, performs the same npm install and Angular build but with `--environment=prod`, commits the image, tags it also as latest, and pushes both the commit‑specific and latest tags. In the test stage add a "test" job that runs npm install, builds the app for homolog with the same Angular command, and then runs `docker run --rm -v $PWD:/app -w /app node:8 ./node_modules/.bin/ng test --code-coverage --single-run` to execute unit tests with coverage. In the deploy stage add a "deploy to homolog" job (master only) using the cdrx/rancher-gitlab-deploy image that runs `upgrade --environment FPAbramo --stack brasilqueopovoquer-empurrandojuntos-dev --service frontend --no-finish-upgrade`, and a "deploy to production" job (tags only) with the same image that runs `upgrade --environment FPAbramo --stack empurrandojuntos-prod --service frontend --no-finish-upgrade`.
